---
title: "4. Robustness Checks"
subtitle: "MY498: Capstone Project 2025-26"
author: "68508"
date: today
format:
    html:
        embed-resources: true
        toc: true
        number-sections: true
        fontsize: 12pt
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(zoo)
library(sf)
library(here)
library(fixest)
library(did)
library(rdrobust)
library(modelsummary)
library(kableExtra)
library(patchwork)

Proc <- here("data", "processed")

analysis_panel <- readRDS(file.path(Proc, "analysis_panel.rds"))
study_sf       <- readRDS(file.path(Proc, "study_sf.rds"))

study_sf <- study_sf |>
    mutate(london = as.integer(str_starts(ons_code, "E09")))

message("Panel loaded: ", nrow(analysis_panel), " rows | ",
        n_distinct(analysis_panel$ons_code), " study units")
```

```{r}
#| label: prep-estimation
#| message: false

panel_est <- analysis_panel |>
    st_drop_geometry() |>
    mutate(
        london          = as.integer(str_starts(ons_code, "E09")),
        borough_id      = as.integer(factor(ons_code)),
        time_id         = as.integer(factor(yq)),
        period          = year * 4 + quarter,
        log_ulev_share  = log(ulev_share + 0.0001),
        log_house_price = log(house_price)
    ) |>
    filter(yq >= as.yearqtr("2019 Q1"))

message("Estimation sample: ", nrow(panel_est), " rows")
message("  London boroughs: ", n_distinct(panel_est$ons_code[panel_est$london == 1]))
message("  Surrounding districts: ", n_distinct(panel_est$ons_code[panel_est$london == 0]))
message("  Quarters: ", n_distinct(panel_est$yq))
```

```{r}
#| label: twfe-did
#| message: false

# =============================================================================
# TWFE DiD specifications
# Treated = outer London boroughs (london == 1, treated == 1)
# Control = inner London boroughs (london == 1, treated == 0)
# =============================================================================

# 1. Baseline — London boroughs only
twfe_base <- feols(
    ulev_share ~ treated:post_announce |
        borough_id + time_id,
    data    = panel_est |> filter(london == 1),
    cluster = ~ons_code
)

# 2. With surrounding districts as additional never-treated controls
twfe_surround <- feols(
    ulev_share ~ treated:post_announce |
        borough_id + time_id,
    data    = panel_est,
    cluster = ~ons_code
)

# 3. With linear trends — London only
twfe_trends <- feols(
    ulev_share ~ treated:post_announce + treated:t_announce |
        borough_id + time_id,
    data    = panel_est |> filter(london == 1),
    cluster = ~ons_code
)

# 4. With linear trends — including surrounding districts
twfe_trends_surround <- feols(
    ulev_share ~ treated:post_announce + treated:t_announce |
        borough_id + time_id,
    data    = panel_est,
    cluster = ~ons_code
)

modelsummary(
    list(
        "Baseline\n(London only)"        = twfe_base,
        "With Surrounding\nDistricts"    = twfe_surround,
        "Linear Trends\n(London only)"   = twfe_trends,
        "Linear Trends\n(+ Surrounding)" = twfe_trends_surround
    ),
    stars   = c("*" = 0.1, "**" = 0.05, "***" = 0.01),
    gof_map = c("nobs", "adj.r.squared", "rmse"),
    title   = "TWFE DiD Results: ULEV Compliance Share"
)
```

```{r}
#| label: twfe-did-baseline
#| message: false

# 1. Baseline — London boroughs only
twfe_base <- feols(
    ulev_share ~ treated:post_announce + log_house_price |
        borough_id + time_id,
    data    = panel_est |> filter(london == 1),
    cluster = ~ons_code
)

# 2. With surrounding districts
twfe_surround <- feols(
    ulev_share ~ treated:post_announce + log_house_price |
        borough_id + time_id,
    data    = panel_est,
    cluster = ~ons_code
)

# 3. With linear trends — London only
twfe_trends <- feols(
    ulev_share ~ treated:post_announce + treated:t_announce + 
                 log_house_price |
        borough_id + time_id,
    data    = panel_est |> filter(london == 1),
    cluster = ~ons_code
)

# 4. With linear trends — including surrounding districts
twfe_trends_surround <- feols(
    ulev_share ~ treated:post_announce + treated:t_announce + 
                 log_house_price |
        borough_id + time_id,
    data    = panel_est,
    cluster = ~ons_code
)

modelsummary(
    list(
        "Baseline\n(London only)"        = twfe_base,
        "With Surrounding\nDistricts"    = twfe_surround,
        "Linear Trends\n(London only)"   = twfe_trends,
        "Linear Trends\n(+ Surrounding)" = twfe_trends_surround
    ),
    stars   = c("*" = 0.1, "**" = 0.05, "***" = 0.01),
    gof_map = c("nobs", "adj.r.squared", "rmse"),
    title   = "TWFE DiD Results: ULEV Compliance Share"
)
```

```{r}
#| label: event-study
#| message: false

panel_est <- panel_est |>
    mutate(
        t_bin = case_when(
            t_announce < -12 ~ -12L,
            t_announce > 8   ~  8L,
            TRUE             ~ as.integer(t_announce)
        ),
        t_bin = relevel(factor(t_bin), ref = "-1")
    )

# Event study — London only
es_london <- feols(
    ulev_share ~ treated:t_bin + log_house_price |
        borough_id + time_id,
    data    = panel_est |> filter(london == 1),
    cluster = ~ons_code
)

# Event study — including surrounding districts
es_surround <- feols(
    ulev_share ~ treated:t_bin + log_house_price |
        borough_id + time_id,
    data    = panel_est,
    cluster = ~ons_code
)

# Extract and plot
extract_es <- function(model, label) {
    broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) |>
        filter(str_detect(term, "t_bin")) |>
        mutate(t = as.integer(str_extract(term, "-?\\d+$")),
               spec = label) |>
        bind_rows(tibble(
            term = "ref", estimate = 0, std.error = 0,
            conf.low = 0, conf.high = 0,
            t = -1L, spec = label
        ))
}

es_coefs <- bind_rows(
    extract_es(es_london,   "London only"),
    extract_es(es_surround, "With surrounding districts")
)

ggplot(es_coefs, aes(x = t, y = estimate, colour = spec, fill = spec)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = -0.5, linetype = "dotted", colour = "black") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 2.5,  linetype = "dotted", colour = "red") +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.15,
                colour = NA) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    annotate("text", x = -0.5, y = Inf, vjust = 2, hjust = -0.1,
             label = "Announcement", size = 3, colour = "black") +
    annotate("text", x = 2.5, y = Inf, vjust = 2, hjust = -0.1,
             label = "Implementation", size = 3, colour = "red") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(
        "London only"                  = "steelblue",
        "With surrounding districts"   = "firebrick"
    )) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
        "London only"                  = "steelblue",
        "With surrounding districts"   = "firebrick"
    )) +
    labs(
        title    = "Event Study: ULEV Compliance Share",
        subtitle = "Treated = outer London boroughs | Reference period = t−1",
        x        = "Quarters relative to announcement (2022 Q4 = 0)",
        y        = "Estimated coefficient",
        colour   = NULL, fill = NULL,
        caption  = "SEs clustered by borough. Borough and quarter FE. Control: log house price."
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
#| label: its
#| message: false

# =============================================================================
# Interrupted Time Series (ITS)
# Tests whether announcement caused level shift or trend change
# Three groups: inner London, outer London, surrounding districts
# =============================================================================

its_data <- analysis_panel |>
    st_drop_geometry() |>
    mutate(
        group = case_when(
            london == 1 & treated == 0 ~ "Inner London (Control)",
            london == 1 & treated == 1 ~ "Outer London (Treated)",
            london == 0                ~ "Surrounding Districts"
        ),
        t = as.numeric(yq) - min(as.numeric(yq)),
        time_since_announce = pmax(0, as.numeric(yq) - 
                                   as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2022 Q4")))
    ) |>
    group_by(yq, group, t, time_since_announce, post_announce) |>
    summarise(
        mean_ulev_share = mean(ulev_share, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

# Run ITS for each group
its_inner <- lm(
    mean_ulev_share ~ t + post_announce + time_since_announce,
    data = its_data |> filter(group == "Inner London (Control)")
)

its_outer <- lm(
    mean_ulev_share ~ t + post_announce + time_since_announce,
    data = its_data |> filter(group == "Outer London (Treated)")
)

its_surrounding <- lm(
    mean_ulev_share ~ t + post_announce + time_since_announce,
    data = its_data |> filter(group == "Surrounding Districts")
)

modelsummary(
    list(
        "Inner London (Control)" = its_inner,
        "Outer London (Treated)" = its_outer,
        "Surrounding Districts"  = its_surrounding
    ),
    stars   = c("*" = 0.1, "**" = 0.05, "***" = 0.01),
    gof_map = c("nobs", "adj.r.squared", "rmse"),
    title   = "Interrupted Time Series Results by Group"
)
```

```{r}
#| label: its-plot
#| message: false

# Generate fitted values separately for each group
fitted_inner <- its_data |>
    filter(group == "Inner London (Control)") |>
    mutate(fitted = predict(its_inner))

fitted_outer <- its_data |>
    filter(group == "Outer London (Treated)") |>
    mutate(fitted = predict(its_outer))

fitted_surrounding <- its_data |>
    filter(group == "Surrounding Districts") |>
    mutate(fitted = predict(its_surrounding))

its_fitted <- bind_rows(fitted_inner, fitted_outer, fitted_surrounding)

ggplot(its_fitted, aes(x = yq, colour = group)) +
    geom_point(aes(y = mean_ulev_share), size = 1.5) +
    geom_line(aes(y = fitted), linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2022 Q4")),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2023 Q3")),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "red", linewidth = 0.6) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(
        "Inner London (Control)" = "steelblue",
        "Outer London (Treated)" = "firebrick",
        "Surrounding Districts"  = "darkgreen"
    )) +
    labs(
        title    = "Interrupted Time Series: ULEV Compliance Share",
        subtitle = "Actual (points) vs fitted ITS model (lines)",
        x        = "Quarter",
        y        = "Mean ULEV share",
        colour   = NULL,
        caption  = "Black dashed = announcement (2022 Q4). Red dashed = implementation (2023 Q3)."
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
#| label: its-did
#| message: false

# DiD-ITS — outer London vs surrounding districts only
# Surrounding districts = baseline (never-treated)
# This isolates the announcement effect specific to outer London
# above the common trend in surrounding districts

its_did_data <- analysis_panel |>
    st_drop_geometry() |>
    filter(london == 1 & treated == 1 | london == 0) |>
    mutate(
        t = as.numeric(yq) - min(as.numeric(yq)),
        time_since_announce = pmax(0, as.numeric(yq) -
                                   as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2022 Q4")))
    ) |>
    group_by(yq, treated, london, t, time_since_announce, post_announce) |>
    summarise(
        mean_ulev_share = mean(ulev_share, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    mutate(
        outer_london = as.integer(london == 1 & treated == 1)
    )

its_did_model <- lm(
    mean_ulev_share ~ t + outer_london +
        post_announce + post_announce:outer_london +
        time_since_announce + time_since_announce:outer_london,
    data = its_did_data
)

summary(its_did_model)
```

```{r}
#| label: its-did-plot
#| message: false

fitted_did <- its_did_data |>
    mutate(
        fitted = predict(its_did_model),
        group  = if_else(outer_london == 1, 
                         "Outer London (Treated)", 
                         "Surrounding Districts (Never-Treated)")
    )

ggplot(fitted_did, aes(x = yq, colour = group)) +
    geom_point(aes(y = mean_ulev_share), size = 1.5) +
    geom_line(aes(y = fitted), linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2022 Q4")),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2023 Q3")),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "red", linewidth = 0.6) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(
        "Outer London (Treated)"              = "firebrick",
        "Surrounding Districts (Never-Treated)" = "darkgreen"
    )) +
    labs(
        title    = "DiD-ITS: Outer London vs Surrounding Districts",
        subtitle = "Actual (points) vs fitted ITS model (lines)",
        x        = "Quarter",
        y        = "Mean ULEV share",
        colour   = NULL,
        caption  = "Black dashed = announcement (2022 Q4). Red dashed = implementation (2023 Q3)."
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
#| label: spillover-test
#| message: false

# Test for spillover effects from earlier expansions
# If surrounding districts accelerated ULEV adoption after 2019 and 2021
# expansions despite never being subject to ULEZ, that's spillover evidence

spillover_data <- analysis_panel |>
    st_drop_geometry() |>
    mutate(
        group = case_when(
            london == 1 & treated == 0 ~ "Inner London (Control)",
            london == 1 & treated == 1 ~ "Outer London (Treated)",
            london == 0                ~ "Surrounding Districts (Never-Treated)"
        )
    ) |>
    group_by(yq, group) |>
    summarise(
        mean_ulev_share = mean(ulev_share, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

ggplot(spillover_data, aes(x = yq, y = mean_ulev_share, colour = group)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
    # 2019 expansion
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2017 Q1")),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2019 Q2")),
               linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # 2021 expansion
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2019 Q2")),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "purple", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2021 Q4")),
               linetype = "dotted", colour = "purple", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # 2023 expansion
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2022 Q4")),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2023 Q3")),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
    annotate("text", x = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2017 Q1")),
             y = Inf, vjust = 1.5, hjust = -0.1, size = 2.5,
             label = "2019\nAnn.", colour = "grey40") +
    annotate("text", x = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2019 Q2")),
             y = Inf, vjust = 1.5, hjust = -0.1, size = 2.5,
             label = "2021\nAnn.", colour = "purple") +
    annotate("text", x = as.numeric(as.yearqtr("2022 Q4")),
             y = Inf, vjust = 1.5, hjust = -0.1, size = 2.5,
             label = "2023\nAnn.", colour = "black") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(
        "Inner London (Control)"              = "steelblue",
        "Outer London (Treated)"              = "firebrick",
        "Surrounding Districts (Never-Treated)" = "darkgreen"
    )) +
    labs(
        title    = "ULEV Adoption Trajectories — Spillover Test",
        subtitle = "All three ULEZ expansion announcements and implementations",
        x        = "Quarter",
        y        = "Mean ULEV share",
        colour   = NULL,
        caption  = "Dashed = announcement. Dotted = implementation."
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
#| label: anticipation-2019-2021
#| message: false

# Focus on pre-2023 period to isolate 2019 and 2021 spillover effects
# Key question: did surrounding districts accelerate after 2019 and 2021
# announcements despite never being subject to ULEZ?

anticipation_data <- analysis_panel |>
    st_drop_geometry() |>
    filter(yq <= as.yearqtr("2022 Q3")) |>  # pre-2023 announcement
    mutate(
        group = case_when(
            london == 1 & treated == 0 ~ "Inner London\n(2019 treated)",
            london == 1 & treated == 1 ~ "Outer London\n(2021/2023 treated)",
            london == 0                ~ "Surrounding Districts\n(never treated)"
        ),
        # Event time relative to 2019 announcement (2017 Q1 = 0)
        t_2019 = (year - 2017) * 4 + (quarter - 1),
        # Event time relative to 2021 announcement (2019 Q2 = 0)
        t_2021 = (year - 2019) * 4 + (quarter - 2)
    ) |>
    group_by(yq, group, t_2019, t_2021) |>
    summarise(
        mean_ulev_share = mean(ulev_share, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

# Plot relative to 2019 announcement
ggplot(anticipation_data, 
       aes(x = t_2019, y = mean_ulev_share, colour = group)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0,    linetype = "dashed", 
               colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 8.25, linetype = "dashed", 
               colour = "red", linewidth = 0.6) +
    annotate("text", x = 0, y = Inf, vjust = 1.5, hjust = -0.1,
             size = 3, colour = "black", label = "2019 Ann.") +
    annotate("text", x = 8.25, y = Inf, vjust = 1.5, hjust = -0.1,
             size = 3, colour = "red", label = "2019 Imp.") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(
        "Inner London\n(2019 treated)"          = "steelblue",
        "Outer London\n(2021/2023 treated)"     = "firebrick",
        "Surrounding Districts\n(never treated)" = "darkgreen"
    )) +
    labs(
        title    = "ULEV Adoption Around 2019 ULEZ Announcement",
        subtitle = "Quarters relative to 2019 announcement (2017 Q1 = 0)",
        x        = "Quarters relative to 2019 announcement",
        y        = "Mean ULEV share",
        colour   = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
#| label: rdd
#| message: false

# =============================================================================
# Spatial RDD — distance to 2021 ULEZ boundary as running variable
# Estimate discontinuity in ULEV share at the boundary (rv_2021 = 0)
# Cross-sections at key dates around the 2023 announcement
# Note: underpowered at borough level (n=33 London boroughs)
# =============================================================================

rdd_dates <- list(
    pre_announce  = as.yearqtr("2022 Q3"),
    announcement  = as.yearqtr("2022 Q4"),
    anticipate_1  = as.yearqtr("2023 Q1"),
    anticipate_2  = as.yearqtr("2023 Q2"),
    implement     = as.yearqtr("2023 Q3"),
    post_1        = as.yearqtr("2023 Q4")
)

rdd_results <- map(rdd_dates, function(target_yq) {
    cs <- panel_est |>
        filter(yq == target_yq,
               london == 1,
               !is.na(rv_2021),
               !is.na(ulev_share))
    rdrobust(y = cs$ulev_share, x = cs$rv_2021,
             c = 0, p = 1, kernel = "triangular")
})

# Extract results
rdd_table <- map_dfr(names(rdd_results), function(name) {
    res <- rdd_results[[name]]
    tibble(
        period    = name,
        estimate  = round(res$coef[2], 5),
        se        = round(res$se[2], 5),
        pvalue    = round(res$pv[2], 4),
        bw_km     = round(res$bws[1], 2),
        n_left    = res$N[1],
        n_right   = res$N[2]
    )
})

rdd_table |>
    kable(
        caption   = "Spatial RDD Results — Distance to 2021 ULEZ Boundary",
        col.names = c("Period", "Estimate", "SE", "p-value",
                      "Bandwidth (km)", "N (inside)", "N (outside)")
    ) |>
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
```

```{r}
#| label: rdd-note
#| message: false

# Check how many boroughs fall within the optimal bandwidth
panel_est |>
    filter(yq == as.yearqtr("2022 Q4"), london == 1) |>
    mutate(in_bw = abs(rv_2021) <= 2.24) |>
    group_by(in_bw, treated) |>
    summarise(
        n        = n(),
        boroughs = paste(la_name, collapse = ", "),
        .groups  = "drop"
    ) |>
    kable(
        caption   = "Boroughs Within Optimal Bandwidth (2.24km)",
        col.names = c("Within BW", "Treated", "N", "Boroughs")
    ) |>
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
```
